channel.title: Finance
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Finance, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Why women’s earnings plateau in their 30s while men’s just keep growing through their 40s: It’s not just motherhood
item.description: She enters the workforce at a disadvantage. By the time she turns 35, it's something structural and the numbers make it impossible to look away.

item.title: Today’s NYT Connections Hints And Answers For Friday, March 6 (#999)
item.description: Looking for today's NYT Connections hints? Some help and the answers for today's game are right here to help keep your streak alive.

item.title: Google Gemini was a deadly ‘AI wife’ for this 36-year-old who resisted its call for a ‘mass casualty’ event before his death, lawsuit says
item.description: “Our models generally perform well in these types of challenging conversations ... but unfortunately AI models are not perfect,” Google said.

item.title: Why SXSW Is Ground Zero For The New Rules Of Human Expression
item.description: AI is the newest tool in a long line of human expression. Ahead of SXSW, Superhuman makes the case that AI should empower creativity, not replace it.

item.title: Is Netflix’s New Rachel Weisz Show ‘Vladimir’ Worth Watching?
item.description: Netflix's big new drama is Vladimir, a miniseries starring Rachel Weisz that combines drama and comedy for an erotic show that seems to be the trend l...

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg, Adam Mosseri’s words used against them in never-before-seen videos airing in addiction trial
item.description: "I think that there’s over 2 billion people on Instagram, which means there are millions of teens on Instagram."

item.title: Time on his side: Michael Dell the real business icon as Icahn the activist recedes from view
item.description: In retrospect, the titanic battle between Dell and Icahn likely marked a turning point in activist investing.

item.title: Today’s NYT Connections Answers Explained For Friday, March 6 (#999)
item.description: Not sure what today's NYT Connections answers are all about? Find out just what the different words in today's grid mean and how they fit together.

item.title: Asia faces an energy shock from the Iran war and a closed Strait of Hormuz, as governments halt exports and draw down stockpiles
item.description: Asian countries like China, Korea and Japan have tried to diversify their energy imports, yet they still depend on oil and gas from the Middle East.

item.title: The Cost Of Convenience In The Age Of AI
item.description: Spam filters have been keeping our inboxes protected for decades. Now they’re losing to AI spam.

item.title: Jobless claims point to declining layoffs. Hiring is another story.
Why Berkshire Hathaway is resuming stock buybacks under new CEO
Economic calendar: Jobless-claims and import-prices data on day’s docket
Stocks tumbling as oil surges and Treasury yields push higher — live updates
Stocks now point to lower open as Iran concerns push Treasury yields upward
USDA credits Trump trade deals as agricultural deficit shrinks, farm sector gains ground
item.description: The USDA reports a drop in the agricultural trade deficit from $43.7 billion to $29 billion in 2026. Officials credit President Donald Trump's trade d...

item.title: Private sector added 63,000 jobs in February, above expectations, ADP says
item.description: The figure reported on Wednesday is above economists’ estimates of an increase of 50,000 jobs and higher than the prior month’s revised reading of a g...

item.title: eBay cuts 800 jobs across company operations just days after dropping $1.2B on trendy Gen Z fashion app
item.description: While eBay might be trying to tailor itself to new customer demographics, the company announced it would be cutting 6%, or 800 roles, across its globa...

item.title: Blue state loses over 180,000 residents in past 5 years as high taxes weigh
item.description: Massachusetts lost about 182,000 residents to domestic out-migration from April 2020 to July 2025, according to a Pioneer Institute analysis of the st...

item.title: FedEx says it will return any tariff refunds to customers, shippers who paid them
item.description: FedEx said it will return any tariff refunds it receives to the shippers and customers who paid them after Trump's IEEPA tariffs were struck down as i...

item.title: Silicon Valley Is Raving About a Made-in-China AI Model
item.description: DeepSeek is called “amazing and impressive” despite working with less-advanced chips.

item.title: Reid Hoffman Raises $24.6 Million for AI Cancer-Research Startup
item.description: The LinkedIn co-founder is starting Manas AI with “The Emperor of All Maladies” author Siddhartha Mukherjee.

item.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
item.description: DeepSeek, SoFi and more in the latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom.

item.title: What to Know About China's DeepSeek AI
item.description: The Chinese upstart says it has trained high-performing AI models cheaply, without using the most advanced chips.

item.title: Can an App Replace a Personal Trainer? I Tested Nearly a Dozen to Find Out
item.description: Apps can add flexibility and freshness to your routine. Just try not to get injured.
